prompt:

<sentence>sentence 10</sentence>
content:
<sentence>The Siamese cat's coat is a striking example of temperature-dependent coloration.</sentence>
<sentence>Its body is a warm, creamy ivory, a stark contrast to its darker extremities.</sentence>
<sentence>These cooler areas, forming the "points," include the ears, face, paws, and tail.</sentence>
<sentence>The darker pigment, melanin, is synthesized more readily in the cooler parts of the cat's body.</sentence>
<sentence>This genetic trait, known as Himalayan or Siamese gene, results in a beautiful gradient of color.</sentence>
<sentence>Kittens are born a pale cream all over, and their points gradually darken as they mature.</sentence>
<sentence>The intensity of the points can also vary with the ambient temperature, becoming darker in cooler environments.</sentence>
<sentence>Some Siamese cats might even develop faint "ghost striping" within their points, a subtle tabby pattern.</sentence>
<sentence>The rich, dark chocolate or seal brown of the points is a defining characteristic of this breed.</sentence>
<sentence>This unique pattern gives the Siamese cat its distinctive and elegant appearance.</sentence>
